name: Mama Ocllo
description: Mama Ocllo, also known as Mama Uqllu, was a significant figure in Incan mythology, revered as a mother goddess and a civilizing force. She is often considered the daughter of Inti, the sun god, and Mama Quilla, the moon goddess, although other accounts vary on her parentage. Mama Ocllo is primarily known as the wife and sister of Manco Capac, the legendary founder of the Inca civilization and the first Sapa Inca. Together, they emerged from Lake Titicaca, sent by Inti to bring order and civilization to the people. Mama Ocllo taught the women of the Andes the arts of spinning, weaving, and domestic skills, while Manco Capac taught the men agriculture and warfare. She is credited with establishing social norms and moral codes, laying the foundation for the Inca Empire's complex social structure. Mama Ocllo's role extended beyond domesticity; she was seen as a protector of women and a symbol of fertility, ensuring the continuation of the Inca lineage. Her influence was deeply intertwined with the creation myth and the establishment of Cuzco as the Inca capital. She is often depicted as a wise and benevolent figure, embodying the virtues of motherhood, nurturing, and cultural advancement, contributing significantly to the Inca worldview and societal harmony. Her legacy remains an integral part of Incan heritage, representing the divine feminine principle and the origins of their empire.
subtitle: Mother Goddess and Civilizer of the Inca

keyMyths.title: Emergence from Lake Titicaca
keyMyths.description: Mama Ocllo and Manco Capac emerged from Lake Titicaca, sent by Inti to civilize the people. They carried a golden staff that would sink into the earth, marking the spot for their capital, Cuzco.

keyMyths.title: Teaching the Women
keyMyths.description: Mama Ocllo taught the women of the Andes the skills of spinning, weaving, and domestic management. These skills were crucial for the development of Inca society and its economic stability.
keyMyths.source: Garcilaso de la Vega, Royal Commentaries of the Incas

keyMyths.title: Establishment of Social Order
keyMyths.description: Together with Manco Capac, Mama Ocllo established social norms and moral codes for the Inca people. This contributed to the creation of a cohesive and well-organized empire.
keyMyths.source: Pedro Cieza de León, Chronicle of Peru

sources.author: Garcilaso de la Vega
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Incan history, culture, and mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Incan history, culture, and mythology.
